items.prompt: What is the core idea of a hybrid annotation pipeline?
choices: Use crowds for large-scale simpler tasks and experts for complex or high-stakes tasks
Use only crowds for every medical and legal item
Use only experts for every product attribute tag
Avoid gold sets and audits entirely
items.explain: Section 11.9.4 balances cost, speed, and quality across crowd and expert roles.

items.prompt: Example 11.25 has experts label a small gold set before crowds label the remainder with expert audits.
items.explain: Expert gold then crowd scale-up with adjudication against gold is the stated pattern.

items.prompt: In Example 11.28, how does active learning route NLP items?
choices: Low-confidence sentences to experts; high-confidence ones to crowds
All sentences to crowds with no expert review
All sentences to experts with no crowd involvement
Random assignment with no confidence scores
items.explain: Active learning protects hard cases by sending uncertain items to experts.

items.prompt: According to Example 11.29, how does a hybrid plan reduce labeling spend?
items.rubric: Crowds for routine boxes; experts for adjudication; keep gold-set quality.
items.sample_answer: Pay crowds for routine boxes and experts for adjudication, cutting spend without dropping gold quality.

items.prompt: Example 11.26 assigns rare lesion labeling to pathologists while crowds tag routine street-scene objects.
items.explain: Crowd objects versus expert pathology splits work by difficulty within the campaign.
